newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news since June 6. The +15% June 1-3 spike was driven by pre-window catalysts (Baird PT raise to $120 from $100, Truist upgrade to Buy at $98, Q1 2026 beat: revenue $405.5M +22% YoY, EPS $0.33; automotive USB-C PD3.1 / Hall-effect product launches announced June 2). Since June 6 the stock has faded with the broader semi pullback sparked by Broadcom's cautious AI outlook; today's -2.9% to $101.62 is sector-driven drift, not a fundamental event.

sources.label: Kavout - What's Driving Diodes' Recent Surge Despite Mixed Q1 2026 Results
Kavout - What Triggered the Recent Semiconductor Sell-Off
Sahm Capital - Diodes expanding into integrated automotive USB-C power and motor control (June 2)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's removal case (weak direct AI-node/HBM/packaging relevance, ~98% YTD crowding) stands; no new evidence since June 6 and today's -2.9% sector-driven fade is too small to restore asymmetry, so no second move is warranted.
verdicts.bull: Q1 momentum (revenue +22% YoY, big operating-leverage swing) and automotive/industrial mix at 44% of revenue support the $2B-by-2028 target; a continued pullback toward the low-90s could rebuild a tradable setup.
verdicts.neutral: A diversified discrete/analog supplier whose edge/power AI exposure is indirect; after the analyst-driven rerating it trades on broad-cycle recovery, not on any AI-node bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Up ~98% YTD with the rerating already delivered by sell-side upgrades; sector-wide AI-sentiment wobble (Broadcom outlook, memory/smartphone softness) can unwind a flow-driven move with no bottleneck moat to defend it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings/guidance confirming margin recovery and automotive/industrial content growth.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding unwind in a broad-cycle name with weak direct AI-compute linkage.

verdicts.rankRationale: Low direct CPO linkage was the basis of yesterday's removal call; nothing since June 6 adds CPO relevance, and a -2.9% drift day after a ~98% YTD run does not change the setup at rank 114.
verdicts.bull: If the analog/discrete cycle keeps inflecting, DIOD's power-management content rides the broader data-center buildout even without a CPO-specific role.
verdicts.neutral: Generic power/discrete exposure to compute infrastructure; no co-packaged-optics design wins, components, or test exposure surfaced in any recent news flow.
verdicts.bear: Zero incremental CPO evidence plus a doubled YTD means it occupies list space better used by genuine optics/packaging bottleneck names; the crowding penalty fully applies.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed design win tying Diodes content into optical-module or CPO power delivery (none observed).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Broad-cycle exposure reverses while CPO-specific names keep compounding, widening relative underperformance.
